result.currentPosition: Hybrid registry with content-addressed definitions, but reuse keyed on a machine-enforced run-context fingerprint with TTL/re-attestation that fails closed on drift; human opt-in carryover removed.
result.position: Hybrid registry remains correct, but content-addressing must bind DEFINITION bytes to a run-context fingerprint (inputs + dependency digests + model id) with machine-enforced expiry and fail-closed re-attestation; bytes alone are necessary but not sufficient for semantic identity.
result.previousPosition: Hybrid registry with content-addressed version-pinned definitions plus signed opt-in lessons off by default, treating nothing-mutable-crosses-runs as sufficient.
proposals: Effective identity = H(definition_hash || sorted(dependency_digests) || model_id || input_schema_digest); cache/warm-start key on this composite.
Machine-enforced expiry/re-attestation: each cached projection carries a TTL+fingerprint; on mismatch/expiry the run fails CLOSED and re-projects.
Replace the empty byte-diff with a behavioral attestation: a small frozen probe set hashed into the fingerprint.
Carryover ONLY as signed promotion-by-commit folded into the fingerprinted source digest; drop the 'off by default' human opt-in.
result.reasoning: Conceding r1-msg-005: a content hash (r1-msg-003) certifies bytes not behavior; byte-identical reuse under upgraded deps/model drifts silently and the promotion diff is empty, while 'off by default' is an erodable human gate. The fix is to widen the identity unit: pin definition_hash into a composite run-context fingerprint so any dep/model/input change mints a NEW effective identity, forcing re-projection or re-attestation. This converts the human gate into a machine gate and keeps warm-start while closing the semantic-staleness hole.

references.comment: Concede: hashes certify bytes not semantics; the byte-identical promotion diff is empty; 'off by default' erodes.
Promotion-by-commit becomes the SOLE carryover path, folded into the fingerprinted source digest.
Zero-residue inspects carried state and cannot detect drift where state is unchanged but deps/model moved.

result.shiftReason: r1-msg-005 showed byte-level content addressing + the zero-residue gate cannot detect semantic drift when deps/model change beneath a stable hash, and that 'off by default' is erodable; I moved from byte-identity + human opt-in to composite fingerprint identity + machine-enforced fail-closed re-attestation.

result.currentPosition: Ephemeral per-run experts with the certified claim narrowed to zero-byte-residue + run-scoped provenance; reuse bound to a run-context fingerprint and failing closed on drift even when the content hash is unchanged.
result.position: Ephemeral per-run experts remain correct, but the honest claim narrows to what the runtime can certify: zero BYTE residue + run-scoped provenance — never 'zero semantic contamination.' The rule certifies only what the runtime can see and binds every reuse to a run-context fingerprint, failing closed on drift even when the content hash is unchanged.
result.previousPosition: Ephemeral projection from a digest-pinned source with an immutable projection cache recovering warm-start, treating content-hash identity as sufficient for safe reuse and zero contamination.
proposals: Narrow the certified claim to 'zero BYTE residue + run-scoped provenance'; disclaim 'zero semantic contamination' as outside runtime attestation.
run-context fingerprint = hash(input slice IDs + dependency digests + model id + projection-source digest); reuse valid ONLY when the fingerprint matches.
Fail closed on any fingerprint mismatch: deps/model changed but content hash identical => invalidate and re-project cold.
Replace 'off by default' human gate with a machine gate: carryover requires a fingerprint-bound promotion record; empty diff under a changed fingerprint is auto-rejected.
Emit the fingerprint as run-scoped provenance so audits distinguish byte-identity from behavioral-identity.
result.reasoning: r1-msg-005 lands: content hashes certify bytes not semantics, byte-identical reuse under upgraded deps/model drifts silently, the reviewable diff is empty, and 'off by default' erodes. I accept the narrowing rather than defend an overclaim. Ephemerality still guarantees no MUTABLE state crosses runs and gives run-scoped provenance the runtime can attest, but it never promised semantic invariance. Make the runtime's observable signal the binding key: certify byte residue + projection lineage, and treat anything unobservable (semantics under changed deps/model) as a drift risk that must fail closed.

references.comment: Accepts the byte-vs-semantics gap; converts silent drift into a fail-closed event keyed on a run-context fingerprint.
My earlier 'immutable projection cache recovers warm-start' was too strong: cache validity must key on the full fingerprint, not the source digest alone.
Keeps ephemeral projection + zero contamination, but restates the guarantee as byte-residue + provenance rather than semantic.

result.shiftReason: r1-msg-005 showed content hashes certify bytes not semantics, byte-identical reuse yields an empty promotion diff, and 'off by default' is an eroding gate — so hash-identity is not a safe reuse certificate. I narrowed the honest claim and re-keyed reuse on a full run-context fingerprint that fails closed on drift the hash cannot see.
